title: murdannia sahyadrica
intro: Murdannia sahyadrica, also known as the Western Ghats Mud banana, is a species of plant in the family Commelinaceae, native to the Western Ghats of India. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and treating respiratory issues. In traditional Indian medicine, Murdannia sahyadrica is used in herbal preparations, including decoctions, infusions, and topical applications, to treat a range of ailments.
